---
title: 'catalog serve-unbound'
---

Resolve a catalog entry, unbind a node, and serve the result as an Arrow Flight
service. Clients stream record batches to the server to drive the computation.

This is the catalog-aware counterpart to the top-level
[`serve-unbound`](../serve-unbound.qmd): instead of pointing at a build
directory, you reference an entry by name or alias and xorq resolves it from the
active catalog.

## Usage

```bash
xorq catalog serve-unbound ENTRY [OPTIONS]
```

| Argument / Option | Description |
|-------------------|-------------|
| `ENTRY` | Catalog entry name or alias to serve (required). |
| `--to-unbind-hash TEXT` | Hash of the node to unbind. |
| `--to-unbind-tag TEXT` | Tag of the node to unbind. |
| `--typ TEXT` | Type of the node to unbind. |
| `--host TEXT` | Host to bind the Flight Server (default: `localhost`). |
| `--port INTEGER` | Port to bind the Flight Server (default: random). |
| `--prometheus-port INTEGER` | Port to expose Prometheus metrics (default: disabled). |
| `-c, --code TEXT` | Inline Ibis code expression applied to `source`. |
| `--fuse / --no-fuse` | Enable/disable catalog source fusion (default: enabled). |
| `--rename-params TEXT` | Rename a parameter: `entry,old_name,new_name` (repeatable). |
| `-p, --params TEXT` | Parameter as `key=value` (repeatable, e.g. `--params threshold=0.5`). |
| `--cache-dir TEXT` | Directory for generated parquet cache files. |

## Example

```bash
# Serve an aliased entry, unbinding by tag
xorq catalog serve-unbound flights-model --to-unbind-tag source_input \
  --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8001

# Bind a runtime parameter
xorq catalog serve-unbound scorer --params threshold=0.5
```

## See also

- [`serve-unbound`](../serve-unbound.qmd) — serve an unbound expression from a build directory
- [`serve-flight-udxf`](../serve-flight-udxf.qmd) — serve a build as a Flight UDXF
- [`catalog run`](run.qmd) — compose and execute catalog entries locally
